text:
*[Bug 17549] When building a package, use inRbuildignore() before copying package contents to Tdir*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=17549>

--- Comment #16 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
Created attachment 3733
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3733&action=edit>
Exclude top-level files first, filter more completely after preparing

Here's a patch that filters top-level entries first, then, after preparing the
package, unlinks all files and directories that must be excluded. It passes the
following tests:

```
setwd(tempdir())
package.skeleton('foo')
unlink('foo/man', recursive = TRUE)
# a package with no directories should build
stopifnot(!<http://file.info|file.info>(list.files('foo', full.names = TRUE))$isdir)
stopifnot(tools::Rcmd('build foo') == 0)

file.symlink('/does/not/exist', 'foo/broken_symlink')
stopifnot(tools::Rcmd('build foo') != 0) # should fail
writeLines('^broken_symlink$', 'foo/.Rbuildignore')
# ignored *top-level* broken symlinks should not fail R CMD build
stopifnot(tools::Rcmd('build foo') == 0)
unlink('foo/broken_symlink')

dir.create('foo/src')
writeLines(c(
 'all:', paste0('\t', c(
  'mkdir -p foo/bar/baz',
  'touch foo/bar/baz/quux'
 ))
), 'foo/src/Makefile')
writeLines('quux', 'foo/.Rbuildignore')
tools::Rcmd('build foo')
(f <- untar('foo_1.0.tar.gz', list = TRUE))
# files created during installation while preparing package should be ignored
as well
stopifnot(!grepl('quux', f))
```

(also: building and checking `Matrix` (except for one test in `matr-exp.R` on
trunk, which is unrelated); building and checking `data.table`)

Should it revert more of r90101 and bring `cp -L` back?

text:
*[Bug 17549] When building a package, use inRbuildignore() before copying package contents to Tdir*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=17549>

--- Comment #17 from Ivan Krylov (<mailto:ikrylov@disroot.org|ikrylov@disroot.org>) ---
Created attachment 3734
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3734&action=edit>
Exclude files to copy, then unlink extraneous files again

Filtering only top-level entries before copying the files would reintroduce
some parts of the problem mentioned in Bug 18186 comment 3. Here's a broken
symlink under `R/`, not a top-level entry:

```
setwd(tempdir())
package.skeleton('foo')

# PR18186 comment 3: ignored broken symlink in R/
dir.create('foo/R')
stopifnot(file.symlink('/does/not/exist', 'foo/R/misc.R'))
writeLines('misc[.]R', 'foo/.Rbuildignore')
stopifnot(tools::Rcmd('build foo') == 0)
```

Not sure if it's something that real-life developers would desire. It's almost
easier to filter the files twice instead of filtering top-level entries first
and the complete list second.

*[Bug 19083] setGeneric("f", \(x) { standardGeneric("x") }) makes generic with class 'nonstandardGenericFunction'*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19083>

--- Comment #4 from Mikael Jagan (<mailto:jaganmn2@gmail.com|jaganmn2@gmail.com>) ---
Created attachment 3735
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3735&action=edit>
prune subclasses of "genericFunction"

To help R contributors thinking about patches for CRAN/BioC packages, this
patch implements the proposal for simplification mentioned in comment 3.

text:
*[Bug 19092] New: `R CMD config --ldflags` is incomplete for `--enable-R-static-lib` on macOS*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19092>

            Bug ID: 19092
           Summary: `R CMD config --ldflags` is incomplete for
                    `--enable-R-static-lib` on macOS
           Product: R
           Version: R-devel (trunk)
          Hardware: Other
                OS: Other
            Status: NEW
          Severity: normal
          Priority: P5
         Component: Mac GUI / Mac specific
          Assignee: <mailto:simon.urbanek@r-project.org|simon.urbanek@r-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:kevinushey@gmail.com|kevinushey@gmail.com>
                CC: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>

Created attachment 3736
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3736&action=edit>
Candidate patch.

On macOS, `R CMD config --ldflags` does not emit a usable link line for a
front-end built against the static `libR.a` (`--enable-R-static-lib`).
The line it prints is missing several libraries that `libR.a` depends on,
and lacks the linker flags needed to pull the whole archive in, so linking
an embedding application against `libR.a` fails outright (or links but then
fails to load R's modules at run time).

This affects only `--enable-R-static-lib` builds on macOS. Shared
(`--enable-R-shlib`)
and framework builds are unaffected, and the change below is a no-op on
non-macOS platforms.

## How to reproduce

Configure and build R as a static library:

```sh
./configure --enable-R-static-lib
make
```

Then ask R how to link a front-end against it:

```sh
R CMD config --ldflags
```

and try to use that line to link any program that embeds R (e.g. a trivial
`Rf_initEmbeddedR()` driver). The link fails with undefined symbols, e.g.:

```
"_pcre2_match_8", referenced from:
    _do_grep in libR.a[..](grep.o)
...
"_libintl_setlocale", referenced from:
    _setup_Rmainloop in libR.a[..](main.o)
...
ld: symbol(s) not found for architecture arm64
```

## Root cause

`STATIC_LIBR` in `etc/Makeconf.in` is built from the *package* flavours of
the library lists rather than the full ones:

```make
STATIC_LIBR = @WANT_R_STATIC_TRUE@ @STATICR1@ "$(R_HOME)/lib$(R_ARCH)/libR.a"
@STATICR2@ \
    $(BLAS_LIBS) $(FLIBS) @R_XTRA_LIBS@ $(LIBINTL) @READLINE_LIBS@ $(LIBS)
```

`$(LIBS)` is `@LIBS_PKGS@` and `$(LIBINTL)` is `@LIBINTL_PKGS@`. On macOS,
configure intentionally trims those two for *package* use:

- `LIBS_PKGS` drops `-lpcre2-8 -ldeflate -lzstd -llzma` (<http://configure.ac|configure.ac>, the
  `darwin*` case around the `AC_SUBST(LIBS_PKGS)`), and
- `LIBINTL_PKGS` is blanked, with the comment *"On a macOS build which uses
  static libraries, libintl_dgettext etc are provided by libR.dylib."*

That assumption is correct for a **shared** `libR.dylib` (it carries those
symbols, so packages need not relink them), but it is exactly wrong for
`libR.a`: there is no `libR.dylib`, so the front-end linking `libR.a` must
provide pcre2/lzma/zstd/deflate and libintl itself. Hence the undefined
symbols.

There is also a second, related gap. On ELF platforms `STATIC_LIBR` wraps
`libR.a` in `--whole-archive`/`--no-whole-archive` (via `STATICR1`/`STATICR2`)
and `MAIN_LDFLAGS` carries `--export-dynamic`, so that all of R's symbols end
up in the executable and are visible to the modules R `dlopen()`s at run time.
macOS sets neither `STATICR1`/`STATICR2` nor `-export_dynamic`, so a static
front-end can be left without symbols that those modules need.

## Fix

Make `STATIC_LIBR` use the full, untrimmed lists, and give macOS the
equivalent of the ELF whole-archive/export-dynamic treatment.

`etc/Makeconf.in`: use `@LIBINTL@`/`@LIBS@` instead of the
`$(LIBINTL)`/`$(LIBS)`
package variants, and drop the spaces around the archive so macOS can use the
no-space `-Wl,-force_load,<archive>` form.

`<http://configure.ac|configure.ac>` (the `darwin*` case): set

```sh
STATICR1="-Wl,-force_load,"
STATICR2=" -Wl,-export_dynamic"
```

## Fix

Make

use the full, untrimmed lists, and give macOS the
equivalent of the ELF whole-archive/export-dynamic treatment.

package variants, and drop the spaces around the archive so macOS can use the
no-space

text:
`-force_load` is the macOS counterpart of `--whole-archive`, and
`-export_dynamic` keeps R's symbols in the dynamic symbol table for the
`dlopen()`ed modules. The existing GNU-ld values just carry their own
surrounding whitespace now (so the shared template works for both linkers):

```sh
STATICR1="-Wl,--whole-archive "
STATICR2=" -Wl,--no-whole-archive"
```

The patch is in `r-static-ldflags.patch` (against `<http://configure.ac|configure.ac>` and
`etc/Makeconf.in`; `configure` is regenerated with autoconf).

## Why this is safe / scope

- `STATICR1`/`STATICR2` are used *only* in `STATIC_LIBR`, and `STATIC_LIBR`
  is emitted only when `WANT_R_STATIC` is true. So nothing outside
  `--enable-R-static-lib` is touched -- shared and framework builds are
  unaffected on every platform.
- On non-macOS platforms the change is a literal no-op: `LIBS_PKGS == LIBS`
  and `LIBINTL_PKGS == LIBINTL` there (the trimming is `darwin*`-only), and
  moving the whitespace into `STATICR1`/`STATICR2` reproduces the identical
  `--whole-archive ... --no-whole-archive` string.
- The only behavioural change is for `--enable-R-static-lib` on macOS, which
  is the broken case.

## Testing

On aarch64-apple-darwin25.5.0:

- Built R with `./configure --enable-R-static-lib` (default shared BLAS).
- `R CMD config --ldflags` now prints a complete line:

  ```
  -Wl,-headerpad_max_install_names ... -Wl,-force_load,<R_HOME>/lib/libR.a \
  -Wl,-export_dynamic -L<R_HOME>/lib -lRblas <FLIBS> \
  -L<prefix>/lib -lintl -Wl,-framework -Wl,CoreFoundation -lreadline \
  -lpcre2-8 -ldeflate -lzstd -llzma -lbz2 -lz -licucore -ldl -lm -liconv
  ```

- A front-end that embeds R (`Rf_initEmbeddedR()` + evaluation) links with
  just `R CMD config --cppflags`/`--ldflags`, runs, and loads R's base
  packages. `otool -L` confirms the binary has no `libR.dylib` dependency
  (R is statically linked in).
- Re-checked that a `--enable-R-shlib` build still produces the same
  `--ldflags` as before (the static branch is not taken).
